response:
Cheaper alternatives to **Anaplan** include **Workday Adaptive Planning**, **Planful**, **Vena Solutions**, **Jedox**, **Pigment**, **Cube**, **Abacum**, **Drivetrain**, and **Farseer**; several of these are explicitly described as lower-cost options in the sources.[1][2][3]

- **Workday Adaptive Planning**: average contract value around **$50,000/year** in one source, versus Anaplan’s average contract value around **$200,000**.[1]
- **Planful**: pricing starts at **$32,000/year** for organizations with annual revenues under €50 million.[1]
- **Vena Solutions**: pricing starts at **$23,000/year** for smaller organizations.[1]
- **Jedox**: pricing starts at about **$26,000/year** on average.[1]
- **Farseer**: contract value starts at **$20,000**, and the source frames it as a lower-cost enterprise FP&A option.[1]
- **Pigment**: described as **5–10x cheaper than Anaplan** for certain mid-market/high-growth use cases.[2]
- **Abacum** and **Drivetrain**: positioned in the sources as cost-effective, easier-to-use alternatives for FP&A, especially for mid-market teams.[2][6]

If your goal is the **lowest-cost practical substitute**, the best fit depends on what you use Anaplan for:
- **FP&A / budgeting / forecasting**: **Planful**, **Vena**, **Workday Adaptive Planning**, **Pigment**, **Drivetrain**.[1][2][3]
- **Spreadsheet-first teams wanting Excel familiarity**: **Vena** or **Cube**.[1][7]
- **More enterprise consolidation needs**: **OneStream** or **Board**, though these may still be relatively expensive compared with mid-market tools.[1][2][4]

If you want, I can narrow this to the **cheapest 3 options** for your company size and use case.
